scope.goal: Design and implement GWT regression coverage for gate and executable-mode behavior.
scope.target: Portable fixture-based tooling tests and real smoke evidence
non_goals: Introduce BDDfy into non-.NET shell fixtures

architecture_decisions: Fixture tests must not mutate real index permanently

steps: Design slice: map acceptance conditions to GWT scenarios and fixture safety
Implementation slice: implement safe temporary-repo fixtures
Document Linux/Unix command and residual CI gap
deferred_items: Linux hosted CI remains optional until explicitly authorized

results.summary: Completed the 17-scenario GWT design and implementation evidence. Fifteen executable fixture tests cover GWT-001 through GWT-015 in synthetic repositories; Windows Git Bash syntax, shell validation, critical, and quick smoke cover GWT-016; the Unix recipe and unauthorized hosted-Linux gap satisfy the explicit GWT-017 handoff without claiming hosted execution.
validation_evidence: 15 fixture tests pass for GWT-001 through GWT-015
temporary Git repositories cover required, advisory, conditional, deferred, CLI, mode, Git-mode, and manifest behavior
real HEAD, porcelain status, and shell index snapshots remain unchanged
real critical mode passed 6/6 required checks
real quick mode passed 6/6 with deferred=1 and not-applicable=1
analyzer template tests passed 47/47 and configuration tests passed 2/2
shell asset, AI context, and workflow artifact validators pass
real full mode intentionally excluded because its advisory helper can inspect product test code
Linux hosted CI remains an explicit unverified gap with a portable command recipe
